title: krigia caroliniana var. leptophylla
intro: Krigia caroliniana var. leptophylla, also known as the Carolina marsh daisy or smallflower ragwort, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
